title: fix play kube can't use infra_image in config file
body: play kube always use the default infra image, this PR allows podman to read it from containers.conf file.

comments.body: Errors look legitimate. It appears that there's an error message printed every time a valid containers.conf isn't present, which is something we can't really guarantee. Error's coming out of c/common - it seems like `config.Default()` is throwing errors when there's no config file present. Should probably make it ignore ENOENT?

It worked, finally.

@rhatdan @mheon could you mirror the default infra image to `quay.io/libpod/pause:3.5`? I am not sure using `k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2` is a good idea or not.

> @chenzhiwei We are hoping to get rid of it altogether, and just ship pause, and create out own image on the fly.

I think @vrothberg wants to work on this.
I think using k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2 is fine, if this starts causing problems we can always fix it.

@Luap99  Is it necessary to add a `--infra-image` flag to play kube?
